title: Two Sum
description: Given an array of integers nums and an integer target, return indices of the two numbers that add up to target.

title: Valid Parentheses
description: Given a string s containing just the characters '(', ')', '{', '}', '[' and ']', determine if the input string is valid.

title: Reverse Integer
description: Given a 32-bit signed integer, reverse digits of an integer.

starterCode.python:
def solve(n):
    if n <= 1: return n
    a, b = 0, 1
    for _ in range(2, n+1): a, b = b, a + b
    return b

title: Merge Two Sorted Lists
description: Merge two sorted linked lists and return it as a sorted list.

starterCode.python:
def solve(l1, l2):
    result = []
    i = j = 0
    while i < len(l1) and j < len(l2):
        if l1[i] < l2[j]: result.append(l1[i]); i+=1
        else: result.append(l2[j]); j+=1
    result.extend(l1[i:])
    result.extend(l2[j:])
    return result

title: Climbing Stairs
description: You are climbing a staircase. It takes n steps to reach the top. Each time you can either climb 1 or 2 steps. How many distinct ways can you climb to the top?

starterCode.python:
def solve(n):
    if n <= 2: return n
    a, b = 1, 2
    for _ in range(3, n+1): a, b = b, a + b
    return b

title: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock
description: Given an array prices where prices[i] is the price of a given stock on day i, return the maximum profit you can achieve.

starterCode.python:
def solve(prices):
    min_price = float('inf')
    max_profit = 0
    for price in prices:
        min_price = min(min_price, price)
        max_profit = max(max_profit, price - min_price)
    return max_profit

title: Contains Duplicate
description: Return true if any value appears at least twice in the array.

title: Valid Anagram
description: Given two strings s and t, return true if t is an anagram of s.

starterCode.python:
def solve(s, t):
    return sorted(s) == sorted(t)

title: Binary Search
description: Given a sorted array nums and target, return index if found, else -1.

starterCode.python:
def solve(nums, target):
    l, r = 0, len(nums)-1
    while l <= r:
        mid = (l+r)//2
        if nums[mid]==target: return mid
        elif nums[mid]<target: l=mid+1
        else: r=mid-1
    return -1
